notes: [sv-client-lifecycle.connect.client_connect | logic] ClientConnect: accept a client as an observer  -  Connect-side server->client channel partly deferred (see connect.fix_client_cvars_welcome row): no clientstuff/FixClientCvars/_teams_available stuffcmds, no Handicap_Initialize-here/model_randomizer, no Send_NextMap. (The version_nagtime arming, the `clientversion` request handler, the MOTD/g_mutatormsg/mutators welcome lines, and playban/chatban ARE now wired.); Player_DetermineForcedTeam / forced-team-otherwise spectator routing IS now wired (ClientManager.cs:220 DetermineForcedTeam), with team pre-assigned via AssignBestTeam for team games.

[sv-client-lifecycle.connect.fix_client_cvars_welcome | logic,values,timing,presentation,liveness] FixClientCvars / SendWelcomeMessage / ClientInit_misc: server->client welcome + cvar push  -  More of the welcome channel exists now: the campaign welcome level-num (SendCampaignWelcome), the non-campaign 'Active modifications:' line (SendMutatorsWelcome → BuildMutatorsPrettyString), the g_mutatormsg server mutator message, the sv_motd MOTD (\n-escapes expanded), and the grappling-hook + offhand-blaster gameplay tips are all surfaced to the connecting client. Still unmodeled: FixClientCvars (cl_jumpspeedcap_min/max, cl_shootfromfixedorigin, prediction/gentle), clientstuff, set _teams_available, Send_NextMap_To_Player, and the ClientInit_misc per-client init entity (armor-blockpercent, damagepush speedfactor, fog, serverflags, g_trueaim_minrange, hook/arc shot origins).; The port pushes a fe

[sv-client-lifecycle.join.join_player | logic,presentation] Join: observer -> live player  -  Join queue + team-selection ordering not modeled: the queued_join TeamBalance_AreEqual two-pass (queued-team dibs first pass + strongest-autoselect-by-skill second pass, player_with_dibs conflict) is absent; the port autoselects via a single connect-time AssignBestTeam.; Campaign Join branch (campaign_bots_may_start / ReadyRestart) only partially modeled (CampaignHostAutojoin opts the host in); the bot-start ReadyRestart trigger is not in ClientManager.Join.; Queue-conflict + queued-player branches still deferred: CENTER_JOIN_PLAY_TEAM_QUEUECONFLICT (depends on the unmodeled wants_join/player_with_dibs queue) and ANNCE_BEGIN for a queued player are not fired; Kill_Notification(CPID_PREVENT_JOIN) clear not modeled.

[sv-client-lifecycle.join.join_allowed | logic,values,presentation] joinAllowed gates  -  MIN_SPEC_TIME + (teamplay && lockteams) + Player_GetForcedTeamIndex==TEAM_FORCE_SPECTATOR + g_playban (ForcedSpectate host hook→Bans.IsPlayBanned) + a gametype ForbidSpawn/late-join gate (LMS lockout, GametypeJoinGate) ARE enforced. Still missing: version_mismatch, g_maxping (ping<=0 too-soon + ping>maxping), and the whole QueueNeeded/queuePlayer/ShowTeamSelection + nJoinAllowed/GetPlayerLimit/g_maxplayers/bots_would_leave capacity gate.; No CENTER_JOIN_PREVENT / CENTER_JOIN_PREVENT_VERSIONMISMATCH / CENTER_JOIN_PLAYBAN / CENTER_JOIN_PREVENT_PING / CENTER_TEAMCHANGE_LOCKED centerprints for blocked joins.

[sv-client-lifecycle.spawn.put_player_in_server | logic] PutPlayerInServer: place + load out a spawning player  -  In-PutPlayerInServer team assignment (bot_forced_team / wants_join>0 SetPlayerTeam / TeamBalance_JoinBestTeam) is not run here — the port pre-assigns the team once at connect, so a mid-match team change or wants_join preference isn't honored on respawn.; wr_resetplayer + reload-all-reloadable-weapons-on-spawn (weapon_load = reloading_ammo) and CL_SpawnWeaponentity per slot are flagged as client/networking concerns and not done server-side.; FixPlayermodel resolves every player+bot to sv_defaultplayermodel (client playermodel cvar not plumbed); faithful stand-in but not the real per-client model. GiveRandomWeapons IS wired (randomCount>0 branch); the ForbidRandomStartWeapons hook is not dispatched.

[sv-client-lifecycle.observer.put_observer_in_server | logic] PutObserverInServer: demote to free-fly observer  -  Still omits: SelectObservePoint / observe-spawn placement (the port keeps the current origin instead), VoteCount/ReadyCount recount, the SPECTATE_WARNING / sv_maxplayers_spectator_blocktime block, CHAT_NOSPECTATORS notice, RemoveGrapplingHooks/Portal_ClearAll, alivetime PlayerStats report, and the is_forced/use_spawnpoint signature (the port has no is_forced/use_spawnpoint params).

[sv-client-lifecycle.observer.observer_think | logic] ObserverOrSpectatorThink: join-on-fire + spectate cycling  -  SpectatePrev (impulse 12/16/19/220-229), the IMP_weapon_drop CAMERA_SPECTATOR cycle, MinigameImpulse handling, the +use clippedspectating MOVETYPE_FLY toggle, and the sv_spectate 2 'spectating not allowed' block are not modeled.

[sv-client-lifecycle.observer.autojoin_delayed | logic] Delayed autojoin for a real client (PlayerPreThink)  -  g_maxping / forced-team (TEAM_FORCE_SPECTATOR) / AutoJoinOnConnection mutator hook / (!teamplay || g_balance_teams) gates deferred. The QC `ping > 0` precondition (delay until centreprints work) is not modeled. sv_spectate and g_campaign holds ARE honored.

[sv-client-lifecycle.frame.pressed_keys | presentation] GetPressedKeys: PRESSED_KEYS stat for the HUD  -  Net delivery to the spectated HUD is still missing: Player.PressedKeys is computed server-side but is NOT in the owner-state net protocol, and PressedKeysPanel.PressedKeysOverride is never assigned — so a SPECTATED player's held keys don't reach the client HUD. The local-player panel works because PressedKeysPanel computes the bitmask client-side from bind state.; GetPressedKeys mutator hook is not fired as a discrete hook here (the race/cts speed-award + Vortex/OkNex velocity-charge consumers are driven from their own per-frame ticks instead).

[sv-client-lifecycle.frame.prethink_misc | logic,presentation,audio,liveness] PlayerPreThink misc: PlayerUseKey edge, SetZoomState, voice sounds, chat bubble  -  UpdateChatBubble (the chatbubble.spr / minigame_busy bubble over a typing or minigame-busy player, ChatBubbleThink + ShowChatBubble hook) is not modeled — no visible 'is typing' bubble.; The PlayerPreThink +use edge dispatch (PlayerUseKey) is not a single ported function: individual +use consumers (buttons/vehicles/ctf) read the button directly, but the usekeypressed edge tracker + PlayerUseKey routing (and its spectate/minigame branches) are scattered/absent.; SetZoomState (server-side weapon-zoom state for the zoom HUD/FOV net stat) + the taunt_soundtime taunt voice + target_voicescript_next are not driven from a lifecycle PreThink. (The deferred teamkill_soundtime friendly-fire complaint voice IS now fired — PlayerFrameLogic.

[sv-client-lifecycle.disconnect.client_disconnect | logic] ClientDisconnect: teardown  -  Still not modeled: ignore-list cleanup, Portal_ClearAll/RemoveGrapplingHooks, chatbubble/killindicator/personal/g_counters delete, minigame part, WaypointSprite_PlayerGone, ReadyCount/VoteCount-on-leave, sv_hook_lastleave, and TeamBalance_RemoveExcessPlayers / QueuedPlayersTagIn. (Vehicle exit, player_powerups_remove_all + poweroff sound, the status-effect clear, the gametype objective-drop, and the generic MUTATOR_CALLHOOK(ClientDisconnect) ARE now modeled.)

[cl-csqcmodel.predraw.dispatch_order | logic,presentation] Predraw dispatch + ordered pass (appearance->LOD->anim->tag->effects)  -  Order is appearance->LOD->effects (skeletal anim + torso-action overlay run in a separate PlayerModel.Pose pass, not interleaved here); the appearance-before-LOD ordering is moot because LOD is a no-op swap.; AutoTagIndex recursion + drawmask=0 on null-model are not part of this pass (held weapons attach via ViewEntityRenderer).

[cl-csqcmodel.fallbackframe.remap | liveness] Fallback frame remap (missing-frame substitution)  -  The runtime frame-id Remap table is live only on the MD3 ModelAnimator player path; the skeletal-IQM player path drives frames by clip NAME (BuildClipTable Pick keyword chains), which BAKE the same fallback discipline (melee->shoot, duckwalk-variant->duckwalk) into the clip resolution rather than remapping a numeric frame id at runtime. So the runtime remap rarely fires for skeletal players, but the substitution behavior is preserved by the keyword cascade.

[cl-csqcmodel.tagindex.weapon_attach | logic,presentation,liveness] Auto tag-index (weapon/attachment bone resolution)  -  The QC h_/v_ model tag resolution cascade (weapon bone -> gettagindex weapon/tag_weapon -> shot/tag_shot, with recursive parent predraw and drawmask=0 when the tag can't resolve) is simplified to a tag_weapon marker attach; weapon-on-weapon and the shot-muzzle tag chain are not faithfully reproduced.; No recursive predraw of the parent entity to fix forcemodel/LOD bone-index mismatch (forcemodel is live, so a forced-model bone-index mismatch on the held weapon attach is a possible edge case; LOD swap is still absent so no LOD-driven mismatch).

[cl-csqcmodel.effects.ef_mf_trail_lights | logic,values,presentation,liveness] EF_*/MF_* effects: dynamic lights, particles, render-flags, trails  -  MF_ROCKET is LIVE (ClientWorld.DriveCsqcModelHooks composes ForcedAppearance(jetpackActive:true) from e.UsingJetpack -> CsqcModelEffects.Apply -> modelFlags|=MF_ROCKET drives both the rocket trail return and the jetpack loop). The OTHER MF trails (grenade/gib/tracer/zomgib/tracer2/tracer3) are mapped in CsqcModelEffectFlags but never supplied — base modelFlags is still 0 — so non-jetpack MF-driven trails never fire on this path.; Strength/Shield role-glow is LIVE via PowerupsMutator (EF_BLUE/EF_RED ORed into player.Effects server-side, networked normally, rendered as the EF_BLUE/EF_RED dynamic lights). The dead-and-redundant code is the unused CsqcModelEffectFlags.RoleGlowFlags helper (colors inverted) + the ComposeForcedAppearance strength

[cl-csqcmodel.networking.csqcmodel_contract | logic,values,presentation,liveness] CSQCMODEL networked-property contract (effects/modelflags/skin/traileffect/colormap/colormod/glowmod/scale/alpha/v_angle/anim_*)  -  Now WIRED end-to-end: alpha (EntityField.Alpha 1<<17), UsingJetpack (NetEntityFlags 1<<8 -> client MF_ROCKET), the upper-body anim ACTION block (EntityField.AnimAction 1<<18 -> UpperAction/AnimActionTime), and the exterior-weapon (wepent) block (EntityField.Wepent -> SwitchWeapon/SwitchingWeapon/WepPhase/ViewmodelSkin/GunAlign/WepAlpha).; STILL NOT networked (unchanged-missing): colormap proper (only a team id is sent), the rest of modelflags (non-jetpack MF_* still 0), traileffect, non-player glowmod, the per-entity colormod (sent for EVERY entity in Base), scale, v_angle, and the anim_state/anim_lower_action LOCOMOTION block (the lower body is inferred client-side, as Base d

[cl-csqcmodel.anim.animstate_set_update | logic,values,timing,presentation] Generic entity frame-animation machinery (anim_set / anim_update animstate)  -  No shared anim_set/anim_update machinery: each consumer re-implements the semantics. Monsters stamp the frame-GROUP start onto the networked Entity.Frame (MonsterAI.DriveAnimFrame + per-descriptor EntityClasses.AnimFrame variant lookup, QC mr_anim/setanim) and the client ModelAnimator plays the group by its own clock — Base's server-side per-tick anim_update frame offset (and the animstate_override one-cycle latch) is not reproduced as state; the override/one-shot discipline is approximated per consumer (e.g. Zombie/Wyvern DeathLanded, ViewModel idle re-assert after a non-looping clip).; EF_RESTARTANIM_BIT is set server-side at spawn (SpawnSystem.cs:868) but consuming it to restart a 1-frame ZYM anim is a no-op on the client (ServerNe

[cl-csqcmodel.animdecide.frame_registry | values,presentation] Player animation framename registry + frame lookup (REGISTER_ANIMATION / ModelAnimation.getframe)  -  Only the canonical framegroup name is matched (substring, case-insensitive): a model whose groups carry only the legacy synonym ('duckstrafeleft') or the ZYM 'groupified_N_anim' names resolves to the groups[0] fallback instead of the right clip. Stock IQM players (erebus/gak/gakmasked) ship canonical names, so default content is unaffected.; dead1/dead2 (static corpse poses) are not distinct picks — the legs Dead clip is a single Pick('death','dead','die').

[cl-csqcmodel.animdecide.explicit_setstate | logic,values,presentation] Explicit networked anim states (animdecide_setstate: DEAD1/DEAD2/DUCK/FROZEN/FOLLOW)  -  No anim_state byte on the wire (see networking.csqcmodel_contract row): the port substitutes discrete flags — Dead + Crouched are networked and live; the die1/die2 LEG distinction is lost (single Dead pose), the torso overlay carries the variant instead.; FROZEN diverges visibly from Base: Base forces the idle clip; the port freezes the current pose under the ice shell (arguably closer to 'encased in ice', but not Base's behavior).; FOLLOW (spectator-follow force-idle) has no counterpart.

[cl-csqcmodel.prediction.csqcplayer_contract | logic,values] Client-side player prediction contract (csqcplayer / csqcplayer_status / cl_movement / stair-smoothing API)  -  cl_movement 0 (turn OFF client prediction, fall back to engine-interpolated local player) has no port counterpart — the local player is always predicted; only the menu dependency binding consumes the cvar.; The smoothing-knob DEFAULTS around this contract diverge on the port path (port cl_movement_errorcompensation=1 vs Base 0 + extra port-only stair knobs) — tracked in depth by the camera/render-smoothing work (see planning notes) and the cl-view smoothing rows; NetGame.cs:5763+ also carries a faithful CSQCPlayer_ApplySmoothing path.

[sv-world-rules.warmup.serverflags | presentation] readlevelcvars serverflags (fullbright / pickuptimer)  -  Only the FORBID_PICKUPTIMER consumer is ported: the HUD pickup panel reads the bit to gate the leading match timer (PickupPanel.cs:169-173, QC client/hud/panel/pickup.qc:91). The ALLOW_FULLBRIGHT consumer is still unported — nothing reads SERVERFLAG_ALLOW_FULLBRIGHT to gate fullbright player rendering (Base client/view.qc:1115 cl_fullbright gate), so that bit has no on-screen effect.

[sv-world-rules.warmup.reset_map | logic] reset_map: full map/player reset on restart  -  No per-client accuracy_reset pass on reset_map (the per-player hit/fire accuracy counters are not zeroed). Inventory_clear is subsumed by the Clients.Spawn -> PutClientInServer start-loadout re-give.

[sv-world-rules.checkrules.cascade | logic,audio] CheckRules_World per-frame win cascade  -  leadlimit / leadlimit_and_fraglimit WIN semantics are still not evaluated in the world layer (the win comes from the gametype MatchEnded latch + tie report). The leadlimit math exists in GameScores.CountFragsRemaining but only feeds the frags-remaining announcer, not the end-of-match decision.; The CheckRules_World mutator hook (M_ARGV status override) is still not invoked.

[sv-world-rules.checkrules.overtime | audio] Overtime / sudden-death (InitiateSuddenDeath/Overtime/GetWinningCode)  -  race/qualifying race_StartCompleting branch deliberately omitted (those modes latch their own winner).

[sv-world-rules.nextlevel.intermission | presentation] NextLevel + intermission entry / map-change timer  -  target_music_kill (stop any playing trigger-music entity at match end) is not invoked here — no MusicKill analogue in the port.; MatchEnd_RestoreSpectatorStatus (move match-joiners back to spectator at match end) is not called here (see new_unmapped).; The bprint team/player 'wins the match' broadcast lines are handled by the notification layer, not this path (verify coverage in sv-notification).

[sv-world-rules.entity.pingplreport | logic,values,timing,presentation,liveness] PingPLReport ping/packet-loss client report  -  Only the packet-loss (PL) half is fed: the port has NO ping feed — the scoreboard ping column is hard -1 (ScoreboardPanel.cs:228/249 'Ping isn't networked per row yet'), and there is no playerstats latency record (the LATENCY_THINKRATE=10s avg-latency accumulation for the xonstat report).; Mechanism diverges: Base uses the TE_CSQC_PINGPLREPORT round-robin entity (one client per think, 3/maxclients s); the port quantizes ENet PacketLoss to a 0..255 byte and folds it into the per-tick scoreboard wire (ScoreRowWire.PacketLossByte), de-quantized client-side for the PL column.

[sv-world-rules.boot.lightstyles | presentation] Animated lightstyle table install  -  Styled lights animate by modulating only the dynlight's own RADIUS (DynamicLightRenderer samples LightStyles.Sample at 10fps and scales OmniRange). DarkPlaces additionally animates WORLD-BRUSH lightmaps for named styles; the port has no lightmap-modulation subsystem, so styled world geometry renders steady (only the dynlight node flickers). (Tracked also under mapobject-misc.yaml.)

[sv-world-rules.misc.redirection | logic,values,timing] RedirectionThink server redirect  -  Only the core quit_and_redirect path is ported (read at match-end map flow, RedirectHandler -> MenuCommand.Connect reconnects the local/listen client). MISSING: the 'self' restart variant (redirection_target == "self" -> disconnect; defer reconnect), the cvar_set sv_public -2 advertise-suppression, the per-second retry loop with redirection_timeout/redirection_nextthink (Base tries twice to catch slow clients), the quit_and_redirect_timer defer delay, and the localcmd quit once all clients are gone.

[sv-world-rules.misc.shutdown | logic] Shutdown: persist DB/bans + slowmo/playerstats/bot teardown  -  ServerProgsDB persistence on shutdown (db_save/db_dump of server.db<sessionid>) has no port analogue.; bot_endgame, CheatShutdown, WeaponStats_Shutdown and MapInfo_Shutdown are not performed on world teardown.

[net-entity-state.csqcmodel.interpolation | presentation] Two-snapshot origin/angle interpolation + teleport/stall snap  -  Animation frame-group cross-fade (cl_lerpanim_maxdelta_framegroups/cl_nolerp) not networked; replaced by client-side LocomotionBlend — remote animation FRAME blending diverges from Base's networked 2-frame lerp. (The separate networked anim ACTION state is tracked in net-entity-state.csqcmodel.anim_state.)

[net-entity-state.replicate.client_cvars | values] Client->server cvar replication (REPLICATE / cmd sentcvar)  -  ONE replicated cvar is stored server-side but not consumed off the replicated value: cl_autoscreenshot is in SentCvarAllowlist (Commands.cs:233) so it is accepted+stored per-client, but nothing on the server reads the REPLICATED value — autoscreenshot is driven CLIENT-locally off the networked intermission flag reading the client's own Api.Cvars cl_autoscreenshot (NetGame.cs), not the per-client replicated store. FIX 2026-06-27: g_xonoticversion left this list — it now has a live server consumer (Commands.CheckClientVersion reads the per-client replicated g_xonoticversion to send the version-mismatch notification, armed at ClientManager.cs:229, fired on the live PlayerPreThink path src/XonoticGodot.Server/GameWorld.cs:2255). FIX 2026-06-25: cl_clippedspectating left this list

[net-entity-state.projectile.fade | logic,values,timing,presentation] Projectile fade-out networking (fade_time/fade_rate bytes, sf 0x20) + client alpha ramp  -  Projectile fade-out is not networked and not rendered: a Base projectile with fade_time/fade_rate set alpha-fades (body + trail) before expiring; the port's NetEntityState carries a per-entity Alpha channel (csqcmodel.alpha) but no projectile producer sets a fade ramp, and ProjectileRenderer has no fade path (grep: no fade symbol) — such projectiles pop out at removal instead of fading.

[net-entity-state.projectile.team_color | values,presentation] Projectile owner team/clientcolors byte (team-tinted bolt body/trail)  -  FFA tint source missing: Base sends the owner's clientcolors when !teamplay so the RocketMinstaLaser bolt tints per-player in FFA; the port networks only e.Team (0 outside teamplay), so the FFA per-player tint never applies.

[net-entity-state.projectile.teleport_resend | logic,values,presentation] Projectile teleport full-resend + no-trail bit (UpdateCSQCProjectileAfterTeleport, warpzone pass-through)  -  No trail discontinuity gate: a projectile passing a warpzone/teleporter draws its per-segment trail (EffectSystem.SpawnTrailSegment) as one straight streak from the entry point to the exit point across the map — Base's 0x08 bit suppresses exactly this. A max-segment-length or Teleported-flag check in ProjectileRenderer.Process would close it.

[net-entity-state.wire.primitives | liveness] Wire serialization primitives (WriteInt24_t, WriteApproxPastTime, Writebits, WriteRegistered, ReadCSQCEntity)  -  WriteApproxPastTime/ReadApproxPastTime are ported (Quantize + BitWriter/BitReader) but DEAD — no caller in game/ or src/ outside the codec files; every networked past-time (AnimActionTime, OrbExpire, respawn times) rides a raw float instead. Already flagged per-consumer (csqcmodel.anim_state values:partial); this row tracks the unused primitive itself.; ReadCSQCEntity (entnum -> entity lookup) has no generic analog; entity references are per-field net-ids (e.g. NetEntityState.Owner) resolved by the consumer.
